---
title-block: false

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: true
    toc-location: left
    anchor-sections: false
    embed-resources: true
    css: styles.css
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    grid: 
      body-width: 1250px
---

```{r}
#
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(here,
               tidyverse,
               tidyr,
               rmarkdown,
               quarto,
               bsicons,
               rlang,
               glue,
               janitor,
               readxl,
               sf,
               knitr,
               kableExtra,
               colorRamps,
               extrafont,
               ggnewscale,
               plotly,
               leaflet)
```


```{r}
#
options(knitr.kable.NA = '--')

here::i_am("Code/sitrep_01.qmd")
```


```{r}
# Date of report generation
datetime_update <- paste0("data as of ", format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y"))

# Date and start time of common event
date_event     <- lubridate::ymd("2026-02-15")
datetime_event <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "15:00:00"))

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates only)
date_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd(date_event - days(3))
date_epicurve_end   <- Sys.Date() + 1

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates and times)
datetime_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "00:00:00"))
datetime_epicurve_end   <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-19", "00:00:00"))
```


```{r}
# Read in linelist
linelist_raw <- readxl::read_xlsx(paste0(here::here(), "/Data/Linelist_Studley_Park_20260220.xlsx"),
                                  sheet     = "Linelist",
                                  skip      = 2,
                                  guess_max = min(100000, Inf)) %>% 
  #
  janitor::clean_names()
```


```{r}
# 
linelist_clean <- linelist_raw %>%
  dplyr::filter(contact_status == "Interview Completed") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    date_of_birth = janitor::convert_to_date(date_of_birth,
                                             character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    age_years = (date_of_birth %--% date_event) / years(1),
    #
    symptom_onset_date = janitor::convert_to_date(symptom_onset_date,
                                                  character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    symptom_end_date = janitor::convert_to_date(symptom_end_date,
                                                character_fun = lubridate::dmy),
    #
    symptom_onset_time = hms::as_hms(symptom_onset_time),
    #
    symptom_end_time = hms::as_hms(symptom_end_time),
    #
    symptom_onset_datetime = lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0(symptom_onset_date, symptom_onset_time)),
    #
    symptom_end_datetime = lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0(symptom_end_date, symptom_end_time)),
    #
    time_to_onset = symptom_onset_datetime - datetime_event,
    #
    symptoms_status = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(symptom_onset_date) & is.na(symptom_end_date)  ~ "Ongoing",
      !is.na(symptom_onset_date) & !is.na(symptom_end_date) ~ "Resolved",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    #
    symptom_end_date = dplyr::case_when(
      symptoms_status == "Ongoing" ~ lubridate::ymd(Sys.Date()),
      TRUE ~ symptom_end_date),
    #
    age_group = factor(dplyr::case_when(
      age_years < 10  ~ "0-9 years",
      age_years < 20  ~ "10-19 years",
      age_years < 30  ~ "20-29 years",
      age_years < 40  ~ "30-39 years",
      age_years < 50  ~ "40-49 years",
      age_years < 60  ~ "50-59 years",
      age_years < 70  ~ "60-69 years",
      age_years < 80  ~ "70-79 years",
      age_years < 90  ~ "80-89 years",
      age_years >= 90 ~ "90+ years",
      TRUE ~ "Not stated"),
      levels = c("0-9 years",
                 "10-19 years",
                 "20-29 years",
                 "30-39 years",
                 "40-49 years",
                 "50-59 years",
                 "60-69 years",
                 "70-79 years",
                 "80-89 years",
                 "90+ years",
                 "Not stated")),
    #
    sex = factor(dplyr::case_when(
      sex == "M" ~ "Male",
      sex == "F" ~ "Female",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
      levels = c("Male",
                 "Female")),
    #
    household = dplyr::case_when(
      residential_address == "27 Ashe crescent Bellfield, 3081"    ~ "Household 01",
      residential_address == "15 Jordan street Ashwood 3147"       ~ "Household 02",
      residential_address == "13 Maypark ave Ashwood 3147"         ~ "Household 03",
      residential_address == "5 Mclelland Way Burwood East 3151"   ~ "Household 04",
      residential_address == "35 Pembroke Street Surry Hills 3127" ~ "Household 05",
      residential_address == "5 Neale St Preston 3072"             ~ "Household 06",
      residential_address == "1/2 Colvin Ct Glen Waverley 3150"    ~ "Household 07",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
```


```{r}
#| out-width: "100%"
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("Images", "nephu_logo.png"))
```

# Situation Report #1

## Outbreak {.unnumbered .unlisted}

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

*Information contained in this Sit Rep is sensitive, confidential, and not intended for wider distribution or use beyond the PH-IMT.*

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

::: {.incident-table}
| Incident name             | Studley Park Boathouse Kew                                        |
|:--------------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------|
| PHESS ID                  | 320266223985                                                      |
| Date/time Sit Rep updated | `r format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y")`                       |
| Sit Rep approved by (IC)  | Claire Gordon                                                     |
| Sit Rep prepared by (IO)  | Alana Little                                                      |

: {tbl-colwidths="[35,65]"}
:::

<div style="height: 1em;"></div>

::: {.callout-note title="Overall summary of incident" icon=false collapse=false}
- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Interviews conducted with 21 attendees across 7 households
  - 16/21 interviewed attendees symptomatic
  - Most common symptoms: anorexia (11/16, 69%), nausea (9/16, 56%), and vomiting (8/16, 50%)
  - No faecal samples collected
  - 10 interviewed attendees across 5 households have agreed to provide faecal samples, relevant Councils arranging sample pot delivery
  - Menu-based questionnaire prepared for distribution to all birthday party attendees

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Boroondara Council notified 18/02/2026 and conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No leftover food available for sampling
  - Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026

:::

<div style="height: 0.125em;"></div>

::: {.callout-tip title="Outbreak case definitions" icon=false collapse=false}
**Confirmed case:** Individuals with a clinically compatible illness who attended the birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026 or who have an epidemiological link to a confirmed case.
:::

***

## Epidemiological Investigations

- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms
  - Noted in interviews that young children became unwell first, followed by parents becoming unwell
  - Epicurve by household suggests secondary household transmission occurring

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Birthday party was held in Perch function room
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - Children consumed a minimal amount of food due to their young age
  - Playpen was used by multiple young children at the party

### Epicurves

**Figure 1** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date.

**Figure 2** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time.

**Figure 3** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and household.

**Figure 4** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and age group.

:::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Figure 1

**Number of people reporting symptoms, by symptom onset date, `r datetime_update`**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_date)) %>%
  #
  count(symptom_onset_date) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(symptom_onset_date, "%d/%m/%Y"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = symptom_onset_date, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 1,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = date_event, xend = date_event, y = 0, yend = max(n, na.rm = TRUE)),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 day",
               date_labels = "%d-%b") +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(date_epicurve_start, date_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.1))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>%
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event date is marked with a dashed purple line.
:::

#### Figure 2

**Number of people reporting symptoms, by symptom onset date and time, `r datetime_update`**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours")) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))
 
figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 10800,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "3 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date and time",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.25,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 2x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 3

**Number of people reporting symptoms, by symptom onset date and time and household, `r datetime_update`**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), household) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Household: ", household, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = household, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.45,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 2x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 4

**Number of people reporting symptoms, by symptom onset date and time and age, `r datetime_update`**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), age_group) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Age: ", age_group, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = age_group, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x    = NULL,
       y    = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.45,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 2x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

::::

***

## Environmental Investigations

- Boroondara Council conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Included deep clean of children’s play area and toilets in the Perch function room

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026
  - Included benches, chopping boards, knives, meat slicer used for platters

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Menu for food served at birthday party and standard menu for Boathouse provided to Council
  - No leftover food available for sampling

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Booking list for day of birthday party and surrounding days provided to Council
  - Further follow-up of booking list not planned at this stage as likely viral gastroenteritis (refer to Risk Assessment section)
  - In addition, booking list consists of >150 individual people who attended on 15/2 so would not be feasible to contact all individuals at this stage
  - Venue does not report illness being reported by other customers
  - Follow up of booking list can be re-visited next week if needed

***

## Risk Assessment

- Likely person-person spread of viral gastroenteritis
  - Minimal food consumption by children at the party
  - Children became unwell before parents
  - Likely secondary spread within households (e.g. Household 04)
  - No common food identified by interviewed cases
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified by Council

***

## Control Measures

- Refer to Environmental Investigations and Risk Assessment sections

***

## Communications

- Disseminate menu-based questionnaire and advice about gastroenteritis to birthday party attendees via host

- Council and DH to be informed via usual processes

- No other broad communications planned at this stage

***

## Further Actions Planned

- Disseminate menu-based questionnaire and advice about gastroenteritis to birthday party attendees via host

- Await collection of faecal samples

```{r}
#
knitr::knit_exit()
```

## Additional Analyses

### Demographics

```{r}
#
linelist_clean %>%
  dplyr::group_by(symptomatic) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n      = as.double(n()),
                   min    = round(min(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   max    = round(max(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   mean   = round(mean(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   median = round(median(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., -Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., NaN))) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrrrrr",
               col.names  = c(" ", "n", "Min", "Max", "Mean", "Median")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:5,
                          width = "1.0in") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 2,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 2, "Age (years)" = 4))
```

### Symptoms

```{r}
#
linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(vomiting       = sum(stringr::str_detect(vomiting, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   diarrhoea      = sum(stringr::str_detect(diarrhoea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   fever          = sum(stringr::str_detect(fever, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   nausea         = sum(stringr::str_detect(nausea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   anorexia       = sum(stringr::str_detect(anorexia, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   headache       = sum(stringr::str_detect(headache, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   lethargy       = sum(stringr::str_detect(lethargy, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  #
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(),
                      names_to = "symptom") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::rename(n = value) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(symptom = stringr::str_to_sentence(symptom),
                symptom = stringr::str_replace(symptom, "_", " ")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::mutate(percent = round(n / (nrow(linelist_clean)) * 100, digits = 0)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::arrange(desc(percent)) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrr",
               col.names  = c("Symptom", "n", "%")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:3,
                          width = "1.0in") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 8,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;")
```
